description: Make the self-hosted goal compiler idiomatic goal, not transpiled Go: (1) finish renaming internal/*.go -> selfhost/*.goal, (2) run `goal fix` across everything to mechanically idiomatize error handling into Result/?, (3) audit each package to use goal idioms (enum, match, sealed interface, derive/from, ...defaults). Gated throughout by behavioral tests, the corpus, and the byte-identical fixpoint. See SELF-HOST-RESEARCH.md and [[goal-selfhost-must-be-idiomatic]].

userStories.title: Rename backend package to selfhost
userStories.description: As the self-host effort, I need internal/backend mirrored into selfhost/backend as goal source so that step 1 (rename) covers the largest package.
acceptanceCriteria: selfhost/backend holds the backend package as .goal files mirroring internal/backend (reserved-word identifier collisions like enum/match/assert renamed as needed)
The transpile-and-build smoke gate compiles selfhost/backend and its ported deps
The existing backend tests (minus those needing repo-relative fixtures) pass against the transpiled package
userStories.notes: Step 1 of 3 (rename). This is the verbatim rename only — idiomatic upgrades come in US-004 (autofix) and US-011 (audit). 3,441 LOC; may need splitting by emit.go/lower.go if it exceeds one context window. token..pipeline are already renamed; backend and typecheck remain.

userStories.title: Rename typecheck package to selfhost
userStories.description: As the self-host effort, I need internal/typecheck mirrored into selfhost/typecheck as goal source so that the rename step is complete for every in-scope package.
acceptanceCriteria: selfhost/typecheck holds the typecheck package as .goal files mirroring internal/typecheck (go/types, go/importer, go/ast, go/parser pass through as foreign imports)
The smoke gate compiles selfhost/typecheck and its ported deps
The existing typecheck depth tests (minus those needing repo-relative fixtures) pass against the transpiled package

userStories.title: Wire self-host main onto ported packages
userStories.description: As the self-host effort, I need the selfhost compiler main to import the selfhost packages (not internal/*) so that the goal-built compiler is genuinely self-contained and the fixpoint covers the whole compiler.
acceptanceCriteria: selfhost/main.goal imports selfhost/{token,lexer,ast,parser,sema,project,pipeline,backend} rather than any internal/* package
The bootstrap builds goal-c-1 and goal-c-2 from the self-contained selfhost tree
task fixpoint passes (goal-c-1 and goal-c-2 emit byte-identical Go for the compiler's own source) and the goal-built compiler passes the corpus transpile + behavioral tiers
userStories.notes: Completes the verbatim self-host: a working goal-built compiler that compiles itself. This is the differential ORACLE for the idiomatic passes that follow — keep it green at every later step.

userStories.title: Run the autofixer across selfhost
userStories.description: As the self-host effort, I need `goal fix` applied to every selfhost package so that manual (T,error) propagation is mechanically idiomatized into Result/? before the hand audit.
acceptanceCriteria: `goal fix --inplace` is run across all selfhost/*.goal and the resulting changes are committed
Re-running `goal fix` over selfhost produces no further changes (the autofix has reached its fixed point)
After the autofix, task check, task build, task fixpoint, and the corpus behavioral tier are all green
userStories.notes: Step 2 of 3 (autofix everything). goal fix auto-converts (T,error)+manual `if err!=nil` into Result[T,error]+`?` (resultsig/propagate rules); it only REPORTS switch->match and never touches enums/interfaces/conversions — those are the US-005+ audit. This story also dogfoods goal fix itself on real compiler code; if it miscompiles, fix that here.

userStories.title: Idiomatic audit: token
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need selfhost/token to use goal idioms so that token kinds are expressed as the language intends.
acceptanceCriteria: iota-based token-kind const blocks are expressed as a goal `enum` where it fits, or the deliberate decision not to is recorded in DECISIONS.md
No manual `if err != nil { return ..., err }` propagation remains that `goal fix` would flag (goal fix reports none for the package)
token tests pass against the transpiled package and task fixpoint stays green
userStories.notes: Step 3 of 3 (idiomatic audit), one package per story. Start small (token) to establish the audit pattern.

userStories.title: Idiomatic audit: lexer
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need selfhost/lexer to use goal idioms so that tokenization reads as idiomatic goal.
acceptanceCriteria: switch statements over an in-file enum are converted to `match`, and fallible helpers return Result/Option with `?` propagation where natural
goal fix reports no remaining auto-convertible propagation sites in the package
lexer tests pass against the transpiled package and task fixpoint stays green

userStories.title: Idiomatic audit: ast
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need selfhost/ast to use goal idioms so that node kinds and traversal are expressed idiomatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Node-kind groups expressed as Go interfaces are evaluated for `sealed interface`/`enum` representation, with conversions or a recorded DECISIONS.md rationale; switch-over-node-kind becomes `match` where it fits
goal fix reports no remaining auto-convertible propagation sites in the package
ast tests pass against the transpiled package and task fixpoint stays green
userStories.notes: Step 3 (audit). Largest idiomatic opportunity (sealed interfaces + match over node kinds) and largest risk — keep the US-003 verbatim oracle as the behavioral reference. May need splitting.

userStories.title: Idiomatic audit: parser
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need selfhost/parser to use goal idioms so that parse error handling uses Result/?.
acceptanceCriteria: Parser functions returning (node, error) use Result with `?` propagation; switch-over-enum becomes `match` where it fits
goal fix reports no remaining auto-convertible propagation sites in the package
parser tests pass against the transpiled package and task fixpoint stays green
userStories.notes: Step 3 (audit). Result/? is the headline idiom here.

userStories.title: Idiomatic audit: sema
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need selfhost/sema to use goal idioms so that resolution and checking use Result/Option and enums.
acceptanceCriteria: Fallible resolution/check functions use Result/Option with `?`; diagnostic/mode kinds expressed as enums + match where they fit
goal fix reports no remaining auto-convertible propagation sites in the package
sema tests pass against the transpiled package and task fixpoint stays green
userStories.notes: Step 3 (audit). 12 files; may need splitting by resolve/check-family/foreign.

userStories.title: Idiomatic audit: project and pipeline
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need selfhost/project and selfhost/pipeline to use goal idioms.
acceptanceCriteria: Fallible discovery/output functions use Result/Option with `?` where natural
goal fix reports no remaining auto-convertible propagation sites in either package
project and pipeline tests pass against the transpiled packages and task fixpoint stays green

userStories.title: Idiomatic audit: backend
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need selfhost/backend to use goal idioms so that emission code reads as idiomatic goal.
acceptanceCriteria: Fallible emit/format functions use Result with `?`; switch-over-node/enum in the emitter becomes `match` where it fits
goal fix reports no remaining auto-convertible propagation sites in the package
backend tests and the corpus transpile + behavioral tiers pass against the transpiled backend, and task fixpoint stays green
userStories.notes: Step 3 (audit). Largest package; may need splitting by emit.go/lower.go.

userStories.title: Idiomatic audit: typecheck
userStories.description: As a goal author, I need selfhost/typecheck to use goal idioms.
acceptanceCriteria: Fallible depth-check functions use Result/Option with `?` where natural
goal fix reports no remaining auto-convertible propagation sites in the package
typecheck depth tests pass against the transpiled package and task fixpoint stays green

userStories.title: Final idiomatic sweep and self-host proof
userStories.description: As the self-host effort, I need a whole-compiler check that no plain-Go patterns remain and the idiomatic compiler still self-hosts, so that the dogfooding is complete and proven.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `goal fix` across the entire selfhost tree reports zero remaining auto-convertible propagation sites, and any deliberately-Go constructs are recorded in DECISIONS.md
task fixpoint passes for the fully idiomatic compiler (goal-c-1 and goal-c-2 byte-identical)
The goal-built idiomatic compiler passes the full corpus transpile + behavioral + check tiers
userStories.notes: Closes the loop: the compiler is written in idiomatic goal AND compiles itself to a byte-identical fixpoint while passing the corpus.
